question.text: An NRI investor redeems equity mutual fund units held for 8 months with a gain of Rs.1,50,000. What is the applicable TDS rate on the capital gain?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For equity-oriented mutual fund units held for less than 12 months, the gain is classified as Short-Term Capital Gain (STCG). STCG on equity is taxed at 15% (under Section 111A). For NRIs, TDS is deducted at this rate (plus surcharge and cess as applicable). The 8-month holding period confirms STCG treatment. **The Trap:** 20% is the TDS rate on IDCW for NRIs, not on STCG from equity. 10% is the LTCG rate for equity gains above Rs.1 lakh. Candidates must distinguish between STCG vs LTCG rates and IDCW vs capital gains TDS. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers capital gains taxation on mutual funds; Series II-B tests the RTA's role in correctly identifying holding period and applying the right TDS rate. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For NRI clients, equity STCG TDS at 15% is unavoidable. If the holding period is close to 12 months, advise waiting a few more days/weeks to convert STCG to LTCG (taxed at 10% above Rs.1 lakh) — the tax savings can be significant on large redemptions.
